title: alnus alnobetula
intro: Alnus alnobetula, also known as green alder or mountain alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to northern regions of North America and Eurasia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to heal wounds, reduce swelling, and fight infections. The medicinal properties of Alnus alnobetula are attributed to the presence of salicylic acid, which is also found in willow bark, and can provide relief from pain, fever, and headaches. Alnus alnobetula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to promote overall health and well-being.
